description: Global glossary for the Danus fact graph: universal math notation shared by ALL projects. A fact's symbols are 'defined' if they appear here, in the per-project fact_graph/glossary.json, in a cited predecessor's glossary_introduces, or in the fact's own glossary_introduces (precedence low->high). See DATA_MODEL.md §3.

Z>=k.definition: The set of integers >= k, i.e. {k, k+1, k+2, ...}; concrete k must be substituted at the point of use (Z>=0 = nonnegative integers, Z>=2, etc.).

N.definition: The natural numbers. Convention: N starts at 0 unless restated locally; use Z+ for {1,2,3,...} when 1-based is required.

R+.definition: The set of strictly positive real numbers, i.e. {x in R : x > 0}.

R>=0.definition: The set of nonnegative real numbers, i.e. {x in R : x >= 0}.

floor(x).definition: For x in R, floor(x) is the unique integer n with n <= x < n+1.
aliases: [x] (when context is integer part, but prefer floor)

ceil(x).definition: For x in R, ceil(x) is the unique integer n with n-1 < x <= n.

{x}.definition: Fractional part: for x in R, {x} = x - floor(x); equivalently, the unique element of [0,1) congruent to x mod 1.

gcd.definition: gcd(a, b) is the greatest common divisor of integers a, b (with gcd(0, 0) = 0 by convention).

lcm.definition: lcm(a, b) is the least common multiple of integers a, b (with lcm(a, 0) = 0).

mod.definition: a mod b (for b != 0) is the unique nonnegative integer r with 0 <= r < |b| and a = qb + r for some integer q.

divides.definition: For integers a, b, 'a divides b' (a | b) means there exists an integer k with b = ka.

[a,b].definition: Closed real interval {x in R : a <= x <= b}. When a, b are integers and the integer interval is meant, restate explicitly as 'integer interval' or use {a, a+1, ..., b}.

(a,b).definition: Open real interval {x in R : a < x < b}.

[a,b).definition: Half-open real interval {x in R : a <= x < b}.

(a,b].definition: Half-open real interval {x in R : a < x <= b}.

iff.definition: If and only if; logical biconditional.

sgn(x).definition: Sign function: sgn(x) = 1 if x > 0, 0 if x = 0, -1 if x < 0.

epsilon.definition: Conventional name for a small real parameter; introduce with an explicit range, e.g. 'let epsilon in (0, 1/Q)'.

eta.definition: Two standard meanings, disambiguated by context: (i) a small positive real parameter (use with explicit range like 'eta in R+, eta < ...'); (ii) the generic point of an integral scheme.

delta.definition: Conventional name for a (small or fixed) real parameter; introduce with an explicit value or range (e.g. 'put delta := deg_C(f^* T_{X/B}) > 0').

lambda.definition: Conventional name for a real or complex parameter / scalar / eigenvalue; introduce with an explicit type (e.g. 'lambda in R+', 'lambda an eigenvalue of A').

mu.definition: Standard meanings include (i) the slope mu(E) := deg(E)/rk(E) of a coherent sheaf on a curve; (ii) a real parameter (introduce with range). Disambiguate by context at the point of use.

nu.definition: Conventional name for a finite covering morphism (e.g. nu : B -> C of degree e), or for a normalization morphism, or sometimes for a real parameter; introduce with the specific role at point of use.

rho.definition: Conventional name for a structure morphism, projection, or scheme map (e.g. rho : W -> B). Introduce the source, target, and any extra structure (projective, dominant, smooth, ...) at the point of use.

sigma.definition: Conventional name for a section of a morphism (e.g. sigma : B -> W is a section of rho : W -> B), or sometimes for a permutation. Introduce the role and source/target at the point of use.

phi.definition: Conventional name for a morphism between schemes / varieties (e.g. phi : W -> X). Introduce source, target, and any further structure at the point of use.

pi.definition: Two standard meanings, disambiguated by context: (i) a structural morphism (e.g. pi : X -> B is a projection or projective morphism); (ii) Archimedes' constant pi = 3.14... in analysis. Introduce the role at the point of use.

tau.definition: Conventional name for a stabilization morphism (Kontsevich), a transposition / permutation, or another auxiliary map. Introduce the role at the point of use.

Phi.definition: Capital phi; conventional name for a Frobenius-type morphism (e.g. geometric Frobenius Phi_m : C_m -> C of degree p^m), or a more general system of morphisms. Introduce the role at the point of use.

Pi.definition: Capital pi; conventional name for a product (Pi_{i in I} a_i), a projection, or another structural map / scheme. Disambiguate at the point of use.

Sigma.definition: Capital sigma; conventional name for a sum, a set, or in algebraic geometry an irreducible curve in a variety (often a rational curve produced by bend-and-break). Disambiguate at the point of use.

Gamma.definition: Capital gamma; conventional name for a graph (e.g. dual graph of a nodal curve), a curve, or a discrete group. Disambiguate at the point of use.

chi.definition: Euler characteristic: for a coherent sheaf F on a projective scheme Y, chi(Y, F) := sum_i (-1)^i h^i(Y, F). On a smooth projective curve C of genus g, chi(C, E) = deg E + rk(E)(1 - g) for a vector bundle E (Riemann-Roch).

h^i.definition: Sheaf cohomology dimension: h^i(Y, F) := dim_k H^i(Y, F) for a coherent sheaf F on a projective k-scheme Y.

X_b.definition: Schematic fiber of a morphism rho : X -> B over a point b in B; defined as X x_B Spec(k(b)) (or X x_B {b} when b is a closed point of B).

O_{Y,y}.definition: Local ring of a scheme Y at a point y; the stalk of the structure sheaf O_Y at y. For a smooth curve B and a closed point b in B, O_{B,b} is a DVR.

T_Y.definition: Tangent sheaf of a scheme Y; on the smooth locus it is a vector bundle of rank dim Y. For a morphism Y -> Z it makes sense to talk about the relative tangent T_{Y/Z}.

T_{Y/Z}.definition: Relative tangent sheaf for a morphism Y -> Z; on the smooth locus of the morphism it is a vector bundle of rank dim Y - dim Z.
